description_long: This is a traditional axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a heavy, wedge-shaped metal head. The handle is made from light-colored wood, showing a natural grain and a gentle ergonomic curve for improved grip and control. The axe head is dark, likely forged steel, with a broad, flat poll and a single sharp cutting edge. The transition between the handle and the head is seamless, with the head securely mounted onto the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This type of axe is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds for chopping wood or other heavy-duty tasks.
description: A traditional axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark, wedge-shaped metal head, designed for chopping tasks. The handle is ergonomic and the head is robust, suitable for urban utility use.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the front, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the broad face of the metal head.
description_view_1: A side profile shows the full length of the handle and the wedge-shaped blade, emphasizing the ergonomic curve.
description_view_2: The back view displays the poll of the axe head and the curvature of the handle, with the blade edge facing left.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile reveals the straight alignment of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, natural finish, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll and no beard; utilitarian and functional with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
